title: Explain Speech Out
short_answer: speech_out is the voice output layer of the app.
text: speech_out receives text meant to be spoken, queues it, tracks playback, stores spoken history, and works with TTS audio output.

title: Explain TTS
short_answer: TTS turns assistant text into spoken audio.
text: TTS is used after the assistant has an answer. It should receive speech_text, generate audio, and let playback state tell the system when the assistant is speaking.

title: Explain Speech Queue
short_answer: speech_queue.jsonl stores text waiting to be spoken.
text: speech_queue is the queue used by the speech output system. It lets the app pass speakable responses to the speaker layer in order.

title: Explain Spoken History
short_answer: spoken_history.jsonl records what the assistant has spoken.
text: spoken_history is useful for debugging, replay awareness, and checking what was actually sent to voice output.

title: Explain Latest TTS
short_answer: latest_tts.json stores the latest generated TTS information.
text: latest_tts helps debug what text was sent to TTS, what audio file was produced, and whether the last TTS step completed correctly.

title: Explain Playback State
short_answer: playback_state.json tells whether assistant audio is playing.
text: Playback state matters because the microphone pipeline should avoid treating the assistant's own voice as new user input.

title: Explain Speaker Status
short_answer: speaker_status.json stores the current status of the speaker layer.
text: Speaker status helps debug whether the speech output system is idle, speaking, blocked, or failing.

title: Explain Speech Audio
short_answer: Speech Audio is the generated audio file used for spoken output.
text: Speech audio files are produced by TTS and played by the speaker layer. They are usually stored under runtime speech output audio folders.

title: Explain Anti Echo
short_answer: Anti Echo prevents the assistant from hearing itself as user input.
text: The app can check playback state or latest spoken text to avoid responding to its own TTS output through the microphone.

title: Explain Voice Output
short_answer: Voice Output is the final spoken response from the assistant.
text: Voice output starts from speech_text, passes through TTS, generates audio, updates playback state, and is heard by the user.
